name: The Royal Portuguese Cabinet of Reading
description: This stunning Brazilian library contains the largest collection of Portuguese texts outside of Portugal itself.

name: Largo do Boticário
description: Crumbling remnant of Rio's colonial past tucked behind Corcovado mountain in the neighborhood of Cosme Velho.

name: Crypt of the São Paulo Cathedral
description: The crypts are so large they're basically their own underground church.

name: Vale da Lua
description: After millennia of erosion by sand and churning water this Brazilian valley now looks like a lunar fantasy.

name: Parque Lage
description: Beautiful public park off the beaten path and away from tourists.

name: Gruta Do Lago Azul (Blue Lake Grotto)
description: Part of one of the largest flooded cavities on the planet, and littered with prehistoric animal bones.

name: Rio de Janeiro Cathedral
description: This massive Brazilian church is built like an angular, technicolor pyramid left by Mayans from the future.

name: Glowing Termite Mounds of Emas National Park
description: These termites have an large and deadly abode—impressive by day, dazzling by night.

name: Biblioteca Nacional do Brasil
description: The largest library in Latin America owes its existence to an Old World earthquake and a Napoleonic invasion.

name: World's Largest Cashew Tree
description: You feel like you're in a forest, but you're actually walking amidst the branches and trunks of a single giant tree.

name: Largest Street Art Mural in the World
description: Eduardo Kobra’s breathtaking 32,300-square-foot graffiti wall took over Rio’s Olympic Boulevard.

name: Lençóis Maranhenses Dunes
description: A faux desert landscape in the middle of the jungle that sprouts oases during the rainy season.

name: Old Petrolandia Church
description: The haunting peak of an old church is all that remains of a drowned Brazilian city.

name: Images of the Unconscious Museum
description: An overlooked collection of over 350,000 artistic works created by psychiatric patients using art therapy.

name: Viaduct Petrobras
description: An abandoned stretch of elevated road in the middle of the Brazilian jungle.

name: Livraria da Vila
description: The bookstore with bookshelves built into unlikely places.

name: Inhotim Museum
description: The largest open-air contemporary art center in South America is a unique experience in the Atlantic Forest.

name: Escadaria Selarón Mosaic Steps
description: A 125-meter long "tribute to the Brazilian people."

name: Cemitério São João Batista
description: Try not to get lost in this picturesque Brazilian labyrinth of the dead.

name: Beco do Batman
description: One of São Paulo's tight urban intersections has become a haven for graffiti artists ever since a single Batman started the trend.

name: Fordlândia
description: Henry Ford's failed rubber plantation in the middle of the Amazon rain forest.

name: Jardim Botânico de São Paulo
description: A jungle in the heart of São Paulo reminds visitors of what Brazil looked like untouched by man.

name: Paranapiacaba Train Station
description: The remains of a British railway rusting in the jungle mist of a Brazilian mountainside.

name: Poço Azul (Blue Pool)
description: Deep below the dry landscape of Bahia, a cave with intense blue water to float in.

name: Chapada das Mesas National Park
description: High plateaus and beautiful waterfalls frame this ecologically significant nature preserve.

name: Snake Island (Ilha da Queimada Grande)
description: Off-limits and full of venomous pit vipers, Brazil’s Snake Island has a frighteningly apt nickname.

name: Gruta Casa de Pedra
description: At 705 feet high, Brazil's "House of Stone" is the largest cave mouth in the world.

name: Brazil's Coffee Palace
description: This majestic building, where Brazil's coffee brokers once bought and sold beans, now houses a coffee museum.

name: Museum of Astronomy and Related Sciences
description: This Brazilian astronomy museum allows guests to gaze at the stars through their telescopes each weekend.

name: Hotel Unique
description: A huge boat-shaped hotel with a blood red pool and other oddities.

name: Parque Nacional Serra da Capivara
description: Site full of incredible prehistoric paintings and archeological findings.

name: Victoria Amazonica
description: Queen of the waterlilies, so big and strong it can support the weight of a human.

name: Victoria Amazonica
description: Queen of the waterlilies, so big and strong it can support the weight of a human.

name: Maned Wolves of Santuário do Caraça
description: This neo-Gothic monastery offers a rare chance to witness the unique maned wolf right up close.

name: Casa de Pedra
description: The eccentric home of Estevão Silva da Conceição, known as the "Brazilian Gaudí."

name: Curitiba Botanical Gardens
description: Solitary monument dedicated to the beauty of nature.

name: Museu da Tatuagem de São Paulo
description: Brazil's only museum dedicated to the art and evolution of tattoos.

name: Niterói Contemporary Art Museum
description: An art museum with an eclectic design and a scandalous past.

name: Giant Anteaters of Serra da Canastra
description: This breathtaking national park is one of the best places to see the weird and wonderful giant anteater in the wild.

name: Valley of the Dinosaurs
description: One of the few places in the world where visitors can walk alongside fossilized tracks from 80 different dinosaur species.

name: Ilha Fiscal
description: This Brazilian palace island was once the site of the last grand ball before revolution.

name: Anchieta Island Correctional Institute
description: The ruins of this Brazilian island prison still bear the scars of the bloody rebellion that closed it down.

name: Nave Espacial de Varginha
description: This spacecraft-shaped water tower is only a part of the mysterious UFO mania in this Brazilian town.

name: Francisco Brennand's Ceramic Workshop
description: The massive gardens and office of a Brazilian sculptor.

name: Museu de Arte de São Paulo
description: This singular art museum in São Paulo is unique inside and out.

name: Cathedral of Brasília
description: A stunning Catholic cathedral built by a curve-loving communist in Brazil’s newly created capital.

name: Insano Body Slide
description: From 135 feet high riders plunge down the world's tallest freestanding body slide at 65 mph.

name: Parque Trianon
description: This park feels like a lush little jungle full of wildlife nestled within São Paulo.

name: Teatro Amazonas in Manaus
description: A beautiful opera house in the heart of Amazonia has recently restarted performances after a 90-year hiatus.

name: Cidade Albanoel
description: An abandoned Santa park is as far as this proposed theme park kingdom got toward completion.

name: Butantan Snake Institute
description: A snake farm famous for its venemous varieties.

name: Walter Galera Garden
description: Hidden in plain sight, this lush garden sits atop one of the most iconic buildings in São Paulo.

name: Jardim do Nêgo (Nêgo's Garden)
description: Moss-covered sculptures fill this small Brazilian gorge.

name: Encontro das Águas (Meeting of Waters)
description: This striking, two-toned confluence of rivers is visible from space.

name: Museu Afro Brasil
description: A labyrinthine museum chronicling the history of African culture in Brazil.

name: Biatüwi
description: The only restaurant serving indigenous foods from the Amazon.

name: National Park of Seven Cities - Parque Nacional de Sete Cidades
description: Unusual rock formations eroded into a landscape of stone beasts.

name: Castelinho da Rua Apa
description: Striking ruins of a mansion that stood center stage in one of modern Brazil's most puzzling crimes.

name: Cachoeira do Lajeado
description: This unique 230-foot waterfall is a natural beauty hidden within the vast wild landscape.

name: Dedo de Deus
description: Known as the "Finger of God," a dramatic rock formation resembles a divine hand extending towards the heavens.

name: Museu Penitenciário Paulista
description: A former prison complex now displays a historic collection of inmate artwork.

name: Museu Oscar Niemeyer
description: Museum precariously perched on a massive yellow pillar.

name: Penedo
description: This "Little Finland" started as a Finnish utopian society in Eastern Brazil.

name: Boca do Acre
description: Hub for hallucinogenic Amazon religion.

name: Museu do Homem do Norte (Northern Man Museum)
description: This anthropological museum offers a glimpse into the lives of the Indigenous peoples of the Amazon River Basin.

name: Museum of Japanese Immigration in Brazil
description: Its collection details the experiences of the largest Japanese community outside Japan.

name: Caxias do Sul Dog Favela
description: A puppy slum that protects canines from the cruelties of the human slums.

name: Árvore dos Desejos (Wish Tree)
description: Brasilia's oldest newsstand is sheltered by a wish-granting tunnel-tree.

name: The Nazi Graveyard of Brazil
description: This gravesite in the Amazon Rainforest was left behind by a failed Third Reich expedition.

name: Bangu Statue of Liberty
description: This Brazilian Lady Liberty is one of the few replicas that was actually made by the original designer.

name: Casa da Flor
description: A self built castle built by a salt worker in Brazil.

name: Ubatuba Whale
description: What killed this humpback whale is a mystery, but its remains have become legendary.

name: Carl Weege Immigrant House Museum
description: The preserved home of the first settler of "the most German town in Brazil."

name: The Capuchin Monkeys of Manduri
description: In the Manduri reserve of Sao Paulo, you can see the endangered black-horned capuchin monkey up close.

name: Cemitério da Consolação
description: This necropolis is home to eclectic funerary art and is the final resting place for much of the city's nobility.

name: Immigration Museum of São Paulo
description: This intriguing museum chronicles the history and impact of immigration in Brazil.

name: Masonic Symbols of Paraty
description: This Brazilian town built by Freemasons is full of colonial Masonic architecture and symbolic imagery.

name: Rua do Amendoim (Peanut Street)
description: Cars mysteriously roll uphill on this street.

name: The Patriarch Tree
description: This magnificent tree towering over the Atlantic rainforest is one of the oldest and largest in Brazil.

name: Zeppelin Hangar at Santa Cruz Air Force Base
description: One of the world's last well-maintained airship hangars.

name: Trilha dos Tucanos
description: This unique bird reserve sits within an important fragment of Brazil's Atlantic rainforest.

name: Fritz Müller Ecology Museum
description: This small Brazilian museum honors the work of a German biologist who studied the jungles for decades.

name: Ricardo Brennand Institute
description: A Tudor-style castle in Brazil holds one of the world's largest armory collections.

name: Theatro Municipal de São Paulo
description: Ella Fitzgerald, opera singer Enrico Caruso, and dancer Isadora Duncan all performed in this lavish, supposedly haunted theater.

name: The Golden Lion Tamarins of Poço das Antas
description: These small, spectacular monkeys are making a comeback after deforestation destroyed much of their rainforest habitat.

name: Itaipu Dam
description: As one of the world's largest hydroelectric dams this plant took two countries to get built.

name: Memorial da Resistência de São Paulo (Memorial of the Resistance)
description: The building that once housed the dictator's secret police is now a museum that documents the history of an oppressive regime and those who fought against it.

name: Pyramids of the Order of the Guardians of the Pharaohs
description: Modern pyramids built to channel positive energy have also fomented local tales of animal sacrifice.

name: São Paulo Pedestrian Signal Lights
description: The pedestrian traffic signals in Brazil's teeming metropolis light up with clues to nearby landmarks.

name: Cachoeiras do Horto (Horto Waterfalls)
description: A trio of waterfalls inside the world’s largest urban forest.

name: Sé Station Mural
description: An enigmatic mural hiding in plain sight in one of São Paulo's busiest metro stations.

name: Geographic Center of South America
description: An obelisk marks the exact center of the continent, as per the calculations of the legendary Brazilian explorer Cândido Rondon.

name: 'Imprensa' Mural
description: A beautiful midcentury mural by the famed Brazilian modernist artist Emiliano Di Cavalcanti.

name: Mundo a Vapor
description: This attraction is devoted to the wonders of steam power and features a replica of one of its most public disasters.

name: The Big Worm
description: This kilometer-long university building is an unexpected architectural delight.

name: Igreja Santa Cruz das Almas dos Enforcados (Church of the Suffocated Souls)
description: Even after multiple failed attempts to be hanged, Chaguinhas was still alive, making people believe it was a miracle.

name: Lagoinha Ruins
description: A place of enchantment where stories of the past seem to come to life.

name: Surfing the Amazon
description: Once a year, ambitious surfers can ride the world's largest river.

name: Vila Maria Zélia
description: The first model village in Brazil, built in 1917 to house workers at the jute sack factory.

name: Monumento Mãe Preta
description: This somber statue remembers the enslaved women who were forced to nurse their owners' babies.

name: Elevador Lacerda
description: This public elevator links the lower city to the upper city of Salvador.

name: Puppet Cabinet at Monteiro Lobato Library
description: Dozens of vintage puppets are displayed in an unassuming hallway at the public library.

name: New Jerusalem Theater
description: World's largest open air theater spans 24 acres and hosts a massive recreation of the Passion of Christ.

name: Casa Coati
description: This modernist building is the last remnant of an ambitious plan to restore a neighborhood.

name: Hidden Madonna Altar
description: A secret Virgin Mary tucked away on a Brazilian beach.

name: 'Monument Tortura Nunca Mais' ('Torture Never Again')
description: This haunting sculpture stands as a memorial to the torture victims of the Brazilian military dictatorship.

name: Superquadra 205/206
description: The most unusual block in Brasília.

name: 'The Indian and the Anteater' Sculpture
description: This mesmerizing statue of a child wrestling an anteater symbolizes the native culture and biodiversity of São Paulo.

name: Fazenda Jambeiro (Jambeiro Farm)
description: A beautiful, abandoned farming headquarters overtaken by nature and graffiti.

name: The Muriquis of Feliciano Miguel Abdala Reserve
description: A preserved section of the Atlantic Forest is an important habitat for these critically endangered monkeys.

name: Praia do Cassino
description: The longest sea beach in the world can be found on the southern coast of Brazil.

name: Martinelli Building
description: This skyscraper once housed weapons during the revolution and was the scene of two brutal crimes that shocked São Paulo society.

name: Ruins of the Ilha Grande Aqueduct
description: The ruins of a 19th-century aqueduct run through this Brazilian island's deep forest.

name: Casa de Dona Yayá
description: This São Paulo home became a lifelong prison for a woman with a tragic childhood.

name: Convento Nossa Senhora da Conceição (Convent of Our Lady of the Conception)
description: This convent has a unique history, including being burned down by a friar trying to get rid of a few bats.

name: Cascata Diamantina (Diamantina Waterfall)
description: A hidden and beautiful waterfall in middle of one of the biggest urban forests of the world.

name: Hans Klussmann Square
description: This sculpture garden creates a whimsical aura in an urban and wild environment.

name: Forte de Santo Antônio da Barra
description: This 17th-century Portuguese fort now houses a nautical museum.

name: Riberão do Meio
description: A natural waterslide in Brazil’s most expansive national park.

name: Leaning Towers of Santos
description: Something about this Brazilian city is slightly askew... literally.

name: Cândido Godói
description: The mystery of Brazil's "Land of Twins" has led to wild theories such as Nazi genetic experiments.

name: Museu do Aleijadinho
description: A display of works by one of the world masters sacred art, an 18th-century Brazilian sculptor nicknamed "the little cripple."

name: Memorial Necrópole Ecumênica
description: In this Brazilian city, a vertical graveyard rules the skyline.

state: State of Rio Grande do Sul
name: Vila Flores Association
description: A group of abandoned factories and buildings that have been revitalized and are now used to promote culture, education, entrepreneurialism, and sustainable development.

name: Morro do Pai Inácio
description: This peak in Chapada Diamantina National Park offers the most stunning sunsets.

name: Positivist Temple of Porto Alegre
description: One of the few remaining temples of the Religion of Humanity.

name: Loja Monstra
description: The craziest comic book shop in São Paulo.

name: Brazil Telephone Museum
description: An emporium of communication in Vitória's Old City.

name: Torre de TV Digital
description: One of the last designs from architect Oscar Niemeyer.

name: Capela Nossa Senhora das Neves
description: After a terrible drought, townspeople used wine to build this church.

name: Marco do Descobrimento (Discovery Landmark)
description: This stone monument marks the beginning of Portugal's presence in Brazil.

name: Mercado Ver-o-Peso
description: This massive waterfront market is located at the "gateway to the Amazon."

name: Torre do Pássaro
description: A giant bird sits atop a tower at one of the largest shopping centers in Brazil.

name: Hermitage of Saint Anthony of Guaibê
description: The scenic ruins of one of the first Catholic churches built in Brazil.

name: Silvana Nakamura Mosaico
description: This artist's studio is decorated inside and out with tiles, mosaics, and ceramic dishware.

name: Basílica Menor de Santo Antônio do Embaré
description: A neo-Gothic church just steps away from the beach.

name: Belvedere do Grinfo
description: This spaceship-like structure was once a popular roadside restaurant.

name: Disneyland of Robots
description: Retro-futuristic contraptions made from recycled materials near a Brazilian resort town.

name: Cabedelo’s Bus-Boats
description: Take a ferry across the Paraíba river in one of these 1980s bus-turned-boats.

name: Maria Bueno's Grave
description: A shrine to a 19th-century femicide victim turned miracle worker.

name: Museu do Seringal
description: This museum on the site of a former rubber plantation showcases the harsh conditions to which rubber tappers in the Amazon were subjected.

name: Darcy Ribeiro Memorial (Beijódromo)
description: This building embodies the vision of Darcy Ribeiro, a renowned Brazilian educator, and founder of the University of Brasília.

name: Lagoa Santa Man
description: The skeletal remains of an ancient human were found with the bones of giant ground sloths.

name: Castelo Armorial
description: A monument dedicated to the Armorial Movement of northeastern Brazil.

name: Edith Gaertner Cat Cemetery
description: The final resting place of the cats that once belonged to a Brazilian actress.

name: Castelo da Barra
description: Tucked away in a beach village, the Castelo da Barra looks like it was plucked straight out of a fairy tale.

name: Box 43
description: Hidden beneath a craft market, this underground cell preserves a physical space from Brazil’s military dictatorship.

name: Casa Bandeirista do Itaim
description: Under the towering glass canopy of Google's headquarters in São Paulo's poshest neighborhood, hides a small, charming 18th-century house.

name: The Gomm House
description: This historical house of a British businessman in Curitiba was saved from destruction by being moved to a new spot.

name: House of the Lion
description: A living folk-art atelier where a Brazilian self-taught artist is building one of the largest lion sculptures in the world.

name: Ariau Towers
description: An Amazonian hotel is the ultimate treehouse and provides stilt based accommodation.

name: Caos Bar & Antiguidades
description: Half curiosity cabinet, half late night hotspot, this Brazilian bar is helping turn a dangerous neighborhood around.

name: Former World's Tallest Lego Tower
description: Many cities have held the title, but Brazil snatched the crown from Chile in 2011.

name: Terra Encantada (Enchanted Land)
description: Plagued by riots, celebrity injuries and a tragic death, this amusement park has been demolished.

name: Ao Rei das Mágicas
description: São Paulo's oldest magic shop has been hiding in plain sight for nearly four decades.
